competitors_analyzed.name: Jim Fan
competitors_analyzed.headline: NVIDIA Director of AI & Distinguished Scientist. Co-Lead of Project GR00T (Humanoid Robotics)
competitors_analyzed.content_strategy: Technical research announcements (60%), robotics demos (30%), thought leadership (10%)
competitors_analyzed.tone: Technical depth made accessible, enthusiastic, research-narrative hybrid

high_performing_posts.text_preview: We trained a humanoid with 22-DoF dexterous hands to assemble model cars, operate syringes, sort poker cards...
What can half of GPT-1 do? We trained a 42M transformer called SONIC to control the body of a humanoid robot...
GR00T N1 is the world's first open foundation model for generalist humanoid robots...

key_patterns: Always leads with the most impressive result or number
Technical depth is accessible — explains the 'why' behind results
Strong use of bullet points and structured formatting

ojas_current_posts.text_preview: AI labs are spending billions on compute. Meanwhile, the data they actually need is locked inside private apps...
99.3% success in simulation. Face-plant by step three on real concrete...
ojas_current_posts.hook_pattern: Dramatic contrast (sim vs real) + specific numbers

ojas_current_posts.text_preview: I just watched a pharma client turn a dead trade show into $2.4M in pipeline...
ojas_current_posts.hook_pattern: Case study with specific revenue numbers + before/after
ojas_current_posts.topic: Robot deployment case study, Gerra showcase

what_works_for_ojas: Story-driven posts with specific numbers (885 likes vs 21-30 for thought leadership)
Case study format with before/after data
Client success stories > abstract thesis posts
what_to_improve: Thesis-level posts need stronger hooks (data moat post only got 21 likes)
Need more personal story content (highest engagement format)
Cross-reference trading returns as proof points in every thesis post
